prevalence.source: Cardiovascular epidemiology syntheses; Brazilian ischemic heart disease context
prevalence.trend: stable to slightly decreasing with risk-factor control, but aging increases absolute cases

incidence.population: Adults at risk for coronary artery disease
incidence.source: Stable ischemic heart disease cohort syntheses

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to disability and MI risk if undertreated
burden.mortalityRate: Low per se; driven by underlying CAD events
burden.source: IHME/GBD ischemic heart disease burden context

vulnerablePopulations: low-income populations with barriers to secondary prevention
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Typical exertional chest pain relieved by rest/nitrates; ECG and stress testing when available; coronary imaging/angiography for high-risk features; rule out ACS

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening for angina; cardiovascular risk screening in primary care varies
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with access to antianginals and secondary prevention

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care recognition with referral for stress testing/cardiology when indicated; access to tests varies across SUS network
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Antianginals (beta-blockers, nitrates, calcium channel blockers), antiplatelet and statin therapy, BP/diabetes control, smoking cessation, cardiac rehab; revascularization for refractory/high-risk cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Unequal access to stress testing and elective angiography, and delayed specialty follow-up in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Secondary prevention medications broadly available as generics; SUS cardiology centers in major cities
